title: Windows makefile returns stange error message if wix is not installed
body:
### Issue Description

The Windows makefile (winmake.ps1) returns a strange error message if Wix is not installed

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Ensure .Net SDK is installed
2. Run `.\winmake.ps1 installer`
3.


### Describe the results you received

```
Building the windows installer for amd64
go build --o "C:\Users\Public\podman\test\version\version.exe" "C:\Users\Public\podman\test\version\"
C:\Users\Public\podman\contrib\win-installer\build.ps1 -Version 6.0.0 -Architecture amd64 -LocalReleaseDirPath "C:\Users\Public\podman\contrib\win-installer\current"
Checking requirements (dotnet and wix)
======================================
C:\Users\Public\podman\contrib\win-installer\build.ps1 : Impossible de trouver un paramètre positionnel acceptant
l'argument «+».
Au caractère Ligne:1 : 1
+ C:\Users\Public\podman\contrib\win-installer\build.ps1 -Version 6.0.0 ...
+ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    + CategoryInfo          : InvalidArgument : (:) [build.ps1], ParameterBindingException
    + FullyQualifiedErrorId : PositionalParameterNotFound,build.ps1
```

### Describe the results you expected

Error message should explicitly state that Wix is missing

### podman info output

```yaml
N/A
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting
